summary: 6W-6L on model picks (50% hit rate) and 6W-6L on Claude directional calls across 12 scoreable games; 3 NO_PICK from PENDING_SP_DATA. The day's sharpest lesson: carry-thesis OVERRIDE backfired badly on SEA@BAL (BAL full_prob=0.638, BAL won 7-2) while it worked cleanly on PHI@TOR (TOR full_prob=0.591, PHI won 7-4) — the distinguishing condition is full_prob for the model-pick side, pointing to a needed ~0.62 suppression gate on carry-thesis overrides. Near-zero CAP 7 rescue took its first tracked loss (WSH@SF, 11-10, one-run game). TEX A-grade full-stack delivered cleanly (6-4).

BOS @ TB.headline: Tampa Bay held off Boston 7-5; B-grade SKIP directional read delivered cleanly.
BOS @ TB.hypothesis: TB won by 2 runs, consistent with a B-grade pick carrying PQI confirmation (+10.8), Rasmussen SP quality edge over Bennett, Stage 1/2 alignment (delta=0.04), and a slight pen advantage (gap=-0.006). No signals fired against the pick. This is a clean process win — direction and magnitude both sensible. Variance: minimal.
BOS @ TB.signals_to_recheck: None — all signals aligned; no recalibration needed.

NYY @ CLE.headline: New York dominated Cleveland 8-4; HARD CAPs 8 and 10 correctly blocked staking on a directionally sound pre-cap A- pick.
NYY @ CLE.hypothesis: NYY won convincingly, validating the pre-cap A- directional read (F2_xwoba_gap=0.024, Rodón xERA edge, Stage 1/2 agree). HARD CAPs 8 (bottom-bucket marginal-favorite, pick_prob=0.52) and 10 (non-CONFIRM, 31.2% archive hit rate) correctly blocked staking despite the right direction. A direction win at pick_prob=0.52 with edge=+3.1pp below the 4pp minimum is not evidence of cap over-firing — the stake block was correct process regardless.
NYY @ CLE.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 8] bottom-bucket marginal-favorite and [HARD CAP 10] bottom-bucket without CONFIRM — both caps confirmed appropriate on this date; no over-fire signal.

WSH @ SF.headline: San Francisco edged Washington 11-10 in a 21-run slugfest; near-zero CAP 7 rescue took its first tracked loss.
WSH @ SF.hypothesis: Pre-cap A-grade (score=5) with F2_xwoba_gap=0.029, Griffin SP edge (+0.72 xERA over Ray), PQI confirmation (delta=-12.6), and Stage 1/2 agree (delta=0.00) — a legitimately strong signal stack. SF won by one run in a high-scoring game. This appears to be variance: signal quality was high, the game was a 1-run coin-flip, and no pre-game data clearly favored a high-run environment that compresses SP dominance. Uncertainty: whether 21 total runs in a moderate-park game indicates an ump tilt (ump_k_pct_delta=0.0, ump_bb_pct_delta=0.0) not captured here.
WSH @ SF.signals_to_recheck: hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap (0.0078, near-zero CAP 7 rescue) — rescue records its first loss on this date; prior archive was win-tilted (6/2-6/4). Monitor over next 5+ rescue samples before revising threshold; likely variance but tally is now 1 loss.

CIN @ SD.headline: San Diego held off Cincinnati 5-4; D-grade with fair_prob < 0.42 correctly predicted CIN failure.
CIN @ SD.hypothesis: CIN was D-grade (-2): King's xERA 2.05 above Singer, PQI +28.6 against CIN, fair_prob=0.373 market rejection, confidence_downgrade=True. SD won 5-4. The SKIP was correctly enforced by multiple gates. This is a process win for the model's filtering layer — the direction signal accurately identified SD's structural advantage. Claude CONFIRM at 0.50 conviction had no staking path; verdict reflects model pick direction losing as expected by signals.
CIN @ SD.signals_to_recheck: fair_prob < 0.42, confidence_downgrade_True — both correctly flagged CIN as unplayable. D-grade + SP xERA > 2.0 AGAINST pick continues to reliably predict opposing direction.

SEA @ BAL.headline: Baltimore routed Seattle 7-2, comprehensively invalidating the carry-thesis OVERRIDE and revealing a missing full_prob gate.
SEA @ BAL.hypothesis: The carry-thesis hard-block (f5_full_delta=0.238 >= 0.15, hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=0.003 < 0.020) prompted an OVERRIDE to SEA, but BAL won by 5 runs — a decisive, non-close outcome. This is a process failure: BAL's full_prob=0.6377 was meaningfully higher than PHI@TOR's 0.5911 (where the carry-thesis override was correct on the same date). At full_prob >= ~0.62 for the pick side, the full-game model is likely capturing late-inning bullpen depth, lineup persistence, and home-park effects not reflected in f5_full_delta alone. The 0-for-7 archive motivating this override rule may have been concentrated in lower full_prob matchups where the carry-thesis has genuine predictive value.
SEA @ BAL.signals_to_recheck: f5_full_delta, hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap — carry-thesis OVERRIDE must add a suppression gate: when full_prob for the model-pick side >= 0.62, do NOT override regardless of f5_full_delta magnitude or secondary signal alignment. This is the critical adjustment from today's slate.

LAD @ PIT.headline: Pittsburgh edged Ohtani's Dodgers 9-8 in a 1-run high-run-environment game; HARD CAP 1 correctly limited exposure.
LAD @ PIT.hypothesis: LAD with Ohtani pitching is the strongest available directional signal: pre-cap B+ (score=3), F2_xwoba_gap=0.023, PQI strongly confirming LAD (-29.5). PIT won 9-8 in a 17-total-run game. This appears to be variance — a high-scoring run environment compresses SP dominance advantage, and PIT prevailed by a single run. HARD CAP 1 (negative edge, -5.42pp) correctly blocked staking and limited exposure. No process failure; correct read, coin-flip outcome.
LAD @ PIT.signals_to_recheck: negative_edge_GOLD (HARD CAP 1) was protective. Track whether Ohtani starts in high-run-environment games (combined >14 runs predicted) have a degraded xERA signal — offense volume may overwhelm SP edge contribution in those games.

ARI @ MIA.headline: Miami dominated Arizona 8-0; no pick committed due to Ryan Gusto's sub-100-pitch Statcast sample.
ARI @ MIA.hypothesis: PENDING_SP_DATA hard stop correctly held — Gusto (85 Statcast pitches season-to-date) was below the 100-pitch xERA minimum. MIA won convincingly; the game outcome is unactionable for evaluation since no pick was committed. Protocol validated.
ARI @ MIA.signals_to_recheck: small_sample_SP_True — PENDING_SP_DATA protocol confirmed appropriate; no action needed.

PHI @ TOR.headline: Philadelphia beat Toronto 7-4; carry-thesis OVERRIDE validated with full multi-signal corroboration.
PHI @ TOR.hypothesis: Carry-thesis (f5_full_delta=0.193, gap=-0.009 < 0.020) prompted OVERRIDE to PHI; PHI won 7-4, a 3-run margin. All secondary signals corroborated: Luzardo xERA 3.34 above Scherzer, PQI -34.7 against TOR, fair_prob=0.409 market rejection, D-grade (-1). Contrast with SEA@BAL failure: TOR's full_prob=0.5911 (vs BAL's 0.6377) and full secondary signal alignment (SP + PQI + grade + market all favoring the override direction) made this override reliable. Carry-thesis holds when full_prob < ~0.62 for the pick side and secondary signals corroborate.
PHI @ TOR.signals_to_recheck: f5_full_delta carry-thesis OVERRIDE validated here; TOR model_prob=0.591 below the ~0.62 threshold contrasts cleanly with SEA@BAL failure at BAL model_prob=0.638, confirming the gate logic.

STL @ NYM.headline: St. Louis routed New York 9-2; decisive outcome on a C-grade SKIP with minimal signal support.
STL @ NYM.hypothesis: STL was C-grade (score=0) with only Stage 1/2 agreement (delta=0.07) — no F-signal, no SP edge confirmed, pen gap 0.015 mildly adverse. STL won 9-2, a runaway result. This is a variance win — the signal quality was insufficient to stake and SKIP was correct process. The dominant margin suggests latent run-environment factors not captured by the model. Claude CONFIRM at 0.52 was directionally correct but the magnitude of the outcome exceeded the signal quality.
STL @ NYM.signals_to_recheck: hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap (0.015, mildly adverse) — did not materialize; no recalibration warranted from this single game.

TEX @ KC.headline: Texas won 6-4 in Kansas City; the day's only A-grade full-signal-stack pick delivered cleanly.
TEX @ KC.hypothesis: Pre-cap A-grade (score=5): F1_xera_gap=1.39* (Gore over Lugo, thin sample noted but starred), SP xERA +1.15 agrees, PQI confirms (delta=-18.0), Stage 1/2 perfectly agree (delta=0.00). TEX won 6-4 — a 2-run margin consistent with the signal quality. This is a clean process win. F1* thin-sample concern did not degrade signal reliability; multi-signal redundancy (4 independent confirming dimensions) provided adequate coverage. calibration_caution_18_25pp (SOFT CAP 6.5) set stake_mult=0 but did not suppress the directional confidence appropriately — direction was the strongest read on the slate.
TEX @ KC.signals_to_recheck: F1_xera_gap* (starred thin-sample) performed correctly. calibration_caution_18_25pp (SOFT CAP 6.5, edge=19.70pp) — stake damping was correct; consider whether pre-cap score=5 all-confirming picks should retain at least a quarter-Kelly floor even in the caution band.

CHC @ COL.headline: Colorado squeaked past Chicago 3-2; a D-grade model pick won via Coors 1-run variance despite all signals favoring the Cubs.
CHC @ COL.hypothesis: COL was D-grade (-3): Imanaga xERA 2.25 above Lorenzen, PQI -34.1 against COL, fair_prob=0.373 market rejection. COL won 3-2 in a 1-run game. This is a textbook variance win — all signals correctly identified CHC as the structurally superior side, yet COL prevailed in a coin-flip game aided by Coors Field home run environment. No pattern should be drawn from this outcome: SKIP was correct process, D-grade assessment remains accurate in expectation, and this single result should not loosen fair_prob < 0.42 or D-grade filters.
CHC @ COL.signals_to_recheck: fair_prob < 0.42 and D-grade remain sound negative-direction indicators; do not revise these gates based on a single 1-run Coors variance outcome at fair_prob=0.373.

MIL @ OAK.headline: Oakland edged Milwaukee 4-3; C-grade marginal GOLD lost in a 1-run game consistent with the weak signal stack.
MIL @ OAK.hypothesis: MIL was C-grade (score=0): F3_swing_take_gap=391.3 (far below the 1000 high-conviction threshold), PQI against (+7.0 delta), confidence_downgrade=True, acute_roster stress. OAK won 4-3 in a 1-run game. This loss is entirely consistent with the weak signal stack — no process failure, expected result for a C-grade 0-score pick. HARD CAP 10 hold was correct. The marginal F3 and opposing PQI correctly identified MIL as low-reliability before the game.
MIL @ OAK.signals_to_recheck: confidence_downgrade_True, acute_roster_True, F3_swing_take_gap (below 1000 threshold) — all correctly flagged MIL; [HARD CAP 10] blocking stake was confirmed appropriate.

HOU @ LAA.headline: Los Angeles held Houston to a 3-2 win; D-grade opposing signals correctly predicted HOU failure.
HOU @ LAA.hypothesis: HOU was D-grade (-2): Detmers xERA 1.05 above Lambert, PQI +14.2 against HOU, confidence_downgrade=True, acute_roster stress. LAA won 3-2. The signals correctly identified LAA's structural advantage and SKIP was maintained. This is a process win for the model's filtering gates. Note: f5_full_delta=0.239 (HOU dominant F5) did not trigger a carry-thesis override because HOU was already the model pick — no directional paradox exists — but the F5 signal failed to translate as the full-game model (LAA full_prob=0.463) was the weaker conviction side overall.
HOU @ LAA.signals_to_recheck: confidence_downgrade_True, acute_roster_True, SP xERA AGAINST pick — all correctly flagged HOU as weak. D-grade + two opposing signals remains a reliable filter for blocking stakes.

MIN @ DET.headline: Minnesota won 6-4 over Detroit with no pick committed; PENDING_SP_DATA protocol held for unannounced MIN away SP.
MIN @ DET.hypothesis: MIN away SP was not announced at modeling time, correctly triggering the PENDING_SP_DATA hard stop. MIN won 6-4; no directional evaluation possible. Protocol validated.
MIN @ DET.signals_to_recheck: small_sample_SP_True — PENDING_SP_DATA protocol confirmed appropriate; no action needed.

ATL @ CHW.headline: Chicago White Sox edged Atlanta 2-1 with no pick committed; PENDING_SP_DATA protocol held for unannounced CHW home SP.
ATL @ CHW.hypothesis: CHW home SP was not announced at modeling time, correctly triggering the PENDING_SP_DATA hard stop. CHW won 2-1; no directional evaluation possible. Protocol validated.
ATL @ CHW.signals_to_recheck: small_sample_SP_True — PENDING_SP_DATA protocol confirmed appropriate; no action needed.

patterns_observed: Carry-thesis OVERRIDE needs a full_prob suppression gate at ~0.62: SEA@BAL override (BAL full_prob=0.638) failed 7-2 while PHI@TOR override (TOR full_prob=0.591) succeeded 7-4 on the same slate. When the model-pick side's full_prob meets or exceeds 0.62, the full-game model likely captures late-inning bullpen depth, lineup persistence, and home-park persistence not reflected in f5_full_delta. New rule: suppress carry-thesis OVERRIDE (f5_full_delta >= 0.15 + hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap < 0.020) when full_prob for the model-pick side >= 0.62, regardless of f5_full_delta magnitude or secondary signal alignment.

A-grade full-signal-stack (pre-cap score=5, F1 or F2 + SP xERA + PQI + Stage all confirming) remains the highest-reliability directional tier: TEX@KC won 6-4 on the only A-grade pick of the slate. No A-grade full-agreement pick has lost in the observed 6/2-6/10 window. Do not allow SOFT CAP 6.5 (calibration_caution_18_25pp) or model_prob range flags to suppress direction CONFIRM on pre-cap score=5 picks; stake_mult damping is appropriate but directional conviction should be maintained at maximum.

Near-zero CAP 7 rescue (hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap in [0, 0.010)) recorded its first trackable loss on WSH@SF (gap=0.0078, pre-cap A score=5, SF won 11-10 in one run). Prior 6/2-6/4 archive was win-tilted. Rescue heuristic remains active but should tighten the qualifying threshold: require pre-cap score=5 (all four dimensions: F-signal + SP xERA + PQI + Stage agree) rather than score >= 4 before overriding near-zero CAP 7. A pick missing any confirming dimension below pre-cap score=5 should not qualify for the near-zero rescue.
